title: The Biggest Animal on Land Is Scared of Something Tiny
script: The largest animal on land is terrified of one of the smallest, and farmers are using it against them. Elephants, animals that fear almost nothing, will flee in a panic from bees. A swarm can sting the soft skin inside their trunk and around their eyes, and an elephant never forgets it. Researchers found that just the recorded buzzing of bees sends whole herds running for cover. So farmers in Africa started hanging beehive fences around their fields, and the elephants simply stopped raiding them. A six-ton giant, outsmarted by an insect. So what else are elephants secretly afraid of?
